page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-180 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the c3d family. It features 180 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 90 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor using x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 354 GB of RAM, providing approximately 1.97 GB of memory per core. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and CPU-based processing.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU scores all placing in the top 10% of tested systems. The AMD EPYC processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16, which contribute to its top-tier performance in extended instructions, encryption, and physics simulations. Memory bandwidth is also highly efficient, with both small-block and large-block read-only bandwidth reaching top-tier levels. However, single-threaded operations, such as gzip compression, perform at average levels.

Given its high core density and compute-to-memory ratio, the c3d-highcpu-180 is designed for highly parallelized, CPU-bound workloads. It demonstrates top-tier efficiency in software compilation, ray tracing, static web serving, and large-scale Redis database operations. Additionally, the instance achieves top-tier prompt processing and text generation speeds for medium and large language models on CPU. The absence of bundled local storage and GPUs makes this instance a cost-efficient choice for massive parallel processing tasks that do not require hardware acceleration or high-speed local disk scratch space.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-180 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 180 dedicated vCPUs and 354 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture with 90 physical cores, this server delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth, placing in the top 10% of benchmarks. It supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AVX512_BF16, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The instance is highly efficient for parallelized, CPU-intensive workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, static web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference. It offers high resource density for multi-threaded applications, making it a cost-efficient alternative to GPU-accelerated instances for specific text generation and prompt processing tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-180 is a compute-optimized instance with 180 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 354 GB RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for parallel workloads and CPU-based LLM inference.

tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized virtual server delivering top-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth for parallelized CPU-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 180 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor
354 GB of RAM optimized for compute-heavy workloads
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth
Supports AVX-512 and AVX512_BF16 instruction sets
Best suited for software compilation, ray tracing, and CPU-based LLM inference
